businessModel.text: Revenue from Innovative Medicine (pharmaceuticals, 45%), MedTech (surgical, cardiovascular, orthopaedic, 35%), and an over-the-counter consumer portfolio post-Kenvue separation (20%).

recentCatalysts.text:
Completed spinoff of Kenvue (consumer health) Q4 2023 — cleaner P&L profile
    Carvykti (cilta-cel) BCMA CAR-T approved Q1 2024, showing strong launch trajectory
    Acquisition of Shockwave Medical (2024) adds $1.1bn coronary IVL franchise
    Pipeline: lazertinib (Lung cancer), andatorysen (solid tumours) in pivotal trials

risks.risk: 1. Stelara (ustekinumab) lost US exclusivity March 2025 — $10bn+ revenue headwind
2. Darwin acquisition (Imedium) adds operational complexity in med-tech adds operational complexity in med-tech
3. Immunology pipeline setbacks could accelerate if FDA scrutinises accelerated approvals
4. Med-tech pricing pressure in US hospital consolidated purchasing groups
5. Talent headwinds in key med-tech markets as integration ofShockwave proceeds

whoShouldOwn.text: Balanced-approach healthcare allocators seeking mid-single-digit total return with lower volatility than pure pharma. Med-tech exposure without owning a separate device name. NOT suitable for investors requiring near-term revenue growth acceleration.

entryExit.text:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) — Entry / Exit Framework

HOLD zone: $219.38–$243.23 — current levels are fairly valued.
Trim / Take Profits above $243.23 — approaching the 52-week high at $251.71.
Stop-loss: A sustained close below $143.2 (52-week low) would warrant reassessment.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
